root_cause: Container log path could not be created or written by the runtime
evidence: Kubelet or runtime reports failure while creating or writing the container log path
Container remains in a waiting startup error state instead of reaching a started state

Dominant log path error: Error: failed to create symbolic link /var/log/containers/orders-api-logpath-failure_default_api-9f0f9d7d2c1e1eab.log to /var/log/pods/default_orders-api-logpath-failure_7d125f65-6487-43bb-96ad-5eab6545cb4f/api/0.log: no space left on device

causes.message: Kubelet asked the runtime to initialize the container log path before startup
The node or runtime could not create or write the container log path
Container cannot finish startup because log initialization failed on the node

pod:orders-api-logpath-failure: Pod cannot start because the runtime could not create or write its container logs

likely_causes: The node filesystem backing /var/log/pods or /var/log/containers is full or quota-limited
Node log storage experienced I/O failures while kubelet or the runtime created container log files
Container log rotation or cleanup did not reclaim enough space on the node log path
suggested_checks: Inspect kubelet and containerd or CRI-O logs for log file or symlink creation failures
Check node disk usage and health for /var/log/pods and /var/log/containers
Review container log rotation and cleanup configuration on the node
